facility_name: Greensparc Edge DC — Cordova Electric Cooperative, Humpback Creek Hydro (Alaska)

opportunity_summary: Greensparc and HPE deployed a 150-170kW modular edge data center at Cordova Electric Cooperative's Humpback Creek Hydro Facility in rural Alaska in spring 2024. Powered by 100% renewable hydroelectric power, it reduces energy costs by up to 80%. The facility is cooled by integration with the hydro plant's cold water. First reliable, powerful computing infrastructure in rural Alaska. Deployed in 30 days.

facility_name: GridFree AI South Dallas One — Hill County, TX (1.5GW+ Grid-Independent AI Power Foundry)
full_address: Hill County, TX (1 hour south of DFW Airport)

cooling_type: Industrial-grade chilled-water cooling; supports high-density compute without depleting local water supplies
opportunity_summary: GridFree AI's South Dallas One is the first development of the South Dallas Cluster — a three-site campus with nearly 5GW cumulative gross power. Each site delivers 1.5GW+. Grid-independent: operates off local electrical grid using American-produced natural gas. Industrial-grade chilled-water cooling for high-density compute. Delivery in <24 months from lease signing. 5x9 availability (99.999%). Newmark as exclusive advisor and marketer.
confidence_reason: PRNewswire, Data Center Knowledge, The Data Center Engineer all confirm. 1.5GW+ per site, chilled-water cooling, grid-independent, natural gas. Newmark exclusive advisor. Early-stage project.

full_address: Jen-Hsun Huang and Lori Mills Huang Collaborative Innovation Complex, Oregon State University, Corvallis, OR
build_phase_if_known: Opening late 2026. $200M facility. NVIDIA Rubin GPUs (150+).
cooling_type: Water-cooled computer racks with heat recovery to 500,000+ sq ft of campus buildings; Kelley District Utility Plant (KDUP) recaptures waste heat; reduces fossil fuel dependence for steam system
opportunity_summary: Oregon State University's $200M Huang Collaborative Innovation Complex opens late 2026 with 150+ NVIDIA Rubin GPUs (next-gen, not yet in production) plus NVIDIA Vera ARM CPUs. ~60 DGX/OVX systems forming a SuperPOD. Water-cooled racks with heat recovery to 500,000+ sq ft of campus buildings via the Kelley District Utility Plant (KDUP). Waste heat reduces campus steam system dependence on fossil fuels. Funded by Jen-Hsun & Lori Huang donation. Designed by ZGF Architects.
confidence_reason: NVIDIA blog, OSU official site, HPCwire, ZGF Architects, OSU Campus Directions blog all confirm. 150+ Rubin GPUs, water-cooled with heat recovery, KDUP integration, late 2026 opening.

facility_name: University of Florida HiPerGator 4th Gen — Gainesville, FL (NVIDIA B200 DGX SuperPOD, Liquid-Cooled)
operator_company: University of Florida / UFIT Research Computing
build_phase_if_known: First NVIDIA DGX B200 servers delivered Jan 2025. Unveiled Oct 2025. Fastest university-owned supercomputer in US.
cooling_type: Liquid cooling systems (confirmed for B200 GPUs at 1200W TDP). Advanced power management tools.
opportunity_summary: University of Florida's HiPerGator 4th Generation is the fastest university-owned supercomputer in the US. Features 63 NVIDIA DGX B200 systems with 504 B200 Tensor Core GPUs, 600 L4 GPUs, ~60,000 cores, and 11PB all-flash parallel file system. B200 GPUs at 1200W TDP require liquid cooling. First DGX B200 servers delivered anywhere — arrived at UF Jan 22, 2025. Unveiled Oct 2025. NVIDIA partnership since 2020 (HiPerGator AI with DGX A100).
confidence_reason: UF official news, UFIT Research Computing, UF Alligator, Times of Innovation all confirm. 504 B200 GPUs, 63 DGX B200 systems, first DGX B200 delivery anywhere. Liquid cooling confirmed for B200 at 1200W TDP.
source_1_title: University of Florida unveils fastest supercomputer in higher education

notes: UNIVERSITY HPC FLAGSHIP. First DGX B200 delivery worldwide. B200 at 1200W TDP requires liquid cooling — NVIDIA's DGX B200 reference design uses 4x2 cold plate loop. UF's NVIDIA partnership dates to 2020. Sustainability-minded build approved with explicit cooling consideration.
current_phase: Operational, fastest university supercomputer in US
pipeline_evidence_snippet: 63 DGX B200 systems, 504 B200 GPUs, 600 L4 GPUs, 60K cores, 11PB flash storage. Liquid cooling. Fastest university supercomputer.
